# chronosphere_consumption_budget (Resource)

A per-partition quota on a consumption_config partition with thresholds that trigger drop, alert, or pause actions when metric or log usage breaches them.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `consumption_config_id` (String) ID of the singleton `chronosphere_consumption_config` resource this budget depends on. Acts as an explicit dependency pointer; the actual partition is selected via `partition_slug_path`.
- `name` (String) Display name of the consumption budget. Can be changed after creation.

### Optional

- `alert_action_config` (Block List, Max: 1) Optional configuration applied to monitors generated by alerting thresholds. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--alert_action_config))
- `default_priority` (Number) Priority assigned to requests that do not match any entry in `priority`. Defaults to 10 (dropped first) if unset.
- `notification_policy_id` (String) ID of the notification policy used to route alerts generated by `ALERT_WARN`/`ALERT_CRITICAL` thresholds. Required only if such thresholds are configured.
- `partition_slug_path` (String) Forward-slash-delimited path identifying the partition the budget applies to (e.g. `global/gateway/dev`). Must start with the `global` partition slug.
- `priority` (Block List) Ordered list of priority rules that classify incoming data. When a drop action runs, higher-numbered priorities (e.g. 10) are dropped first and priority 1 is dropped last. Evaluation stops at the first matching priority. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--priority))
- `resource` (String, Deprecated) Deprecated: use `threshold.sku_group` and `threshold.unit` instead. Identifies the resource type the budget governs.
- `slug` (String) Stable identifier for the consumption budget. Generated from `name` if omitted. Immutable after creation.
- `threshold` (Block List) Threshold actions to take when budget consumption crosses configured levels. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--threshold))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--alert_action_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `alert_action_config`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Additional annotations to set on the generated monitor. Overrides the default `description`, `dashboard`, `resource`, `consumption_budget_slug`, `threshold_type`, and `partition` annotations when keys collide.
- `instant_rate_sustain_secs` (Number) How long instant-rate consumption must remain above the threshold before an alert fires, in seconds. Defaults to 0 (alert immediately on any breach).
- `labels` (Map of String) Additional labels to set on the generated monitor, usable for notification routing. The `resource`, `partition`, and `threshold_type` labels are reserved and cannot be overridden.


<a id="nestedblock--priority"></a>
### Nested Schema for `priority`

Optional:

- `filter` (Block List) Filters identifying which data matches this priority. Filters are AND-ed together: a request must match every filter to be assigned this priority. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--priority--filter))
- `priority` (Number) Priority order used when dropping data. Priority `10` is dropped first; priority `1` is dropped last.

<a id="nestedblock--priority--filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `priority.filter`

Optional:

- `dataset_id` (String) Deprecated: use `log_filter` instead. Slug of the dataset to match against.
- `log_filter` (Block List, Max: 1) Log search filter that matches log data for this priority. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--priority--filter--log_filter))

<a id="nestedblock--priority--filter--log_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `priority.filter.log_filter`

Required:

- `query` (String) Log search query that selects matching logs. Supports only top-level operations; nested clauses are not allowed and only one type of `AND` or `OR` operator can be used.




<a id="nestedblock--threshold"></a>
### Nested Schema for `threshold`

Optional:

- `action` (String) Action to take when this threshold is exceeded (e.g. drop traffic, fire warning/critical alert).
- `instant_rate` (Block List, Max: 1) Configures an instant-rate threshold value. Set when `type` is an instant-rate type. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--threshold--instant_rate))
- `resource_group` (String) Resource group the threshold applies to (e.g. metrics, logs, traces). Replaces the deprecated top-level `resource` field.
- `sku_group` (String, Deprecated) SKU group the threshold applies to (e.g. metrics, logs, traces). Replaces the deprecated top-level `resource` field.
- `type` (String) Measurement window over which the threshold is evaluated (e.g. instant rate vs. rolling volume).
- `unit` (String) Unit in which the threshold value is denominated (e.g. bytes, datapoints).
- `volume` (Block List, Max: 1) Configures a volume threshold value. Set when `type` is a volume type. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--threshold--volume))

<a id="nestedblock--threshold--instant_rate"></a>
### Nested Schema for `threshold.instant_rate`

Optional:

- `fixed_value_per_sec` (Number) Fixed per-second rate threshold value, expressed in the threshold's `unit`.


<a id="nestedblock--threshold--volume"></a>
### Nested Schema for `threshold.volume`

Optional:

- `fixed_value` (Number) Fixed volume threshold value, expressed in the threshold's `unit`.
